sections.title: A: Use the word given in capitals to form a word
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap.

items.sentence: I've always wanted to be an (1) ______ (ENTERTAIN) but never did anything about it until I lost my job last year.

After a (2) ______ (CONVERSE) with a friend about the (3) ______ (BORE) of office life, I decided not to look for another job in management, but to earn my living from giving (4) ______ (PERFORM) at events like kids' parties.
So I became a clown! I'm (5) ______ (CURRENT) doing four or five parties a week and I love it!
I love seeing the (6) ______ (EXCITE) on the kids' faces when I walk in the room.

I try to have a lot of (7) ______ (VARY) in my act, so I tell jokes - most of which the kids find (8) ______ (AMUSE) - and bend balloons into funny animals, do impersonations of (9) ______ (FAME) people and generally keep the kids (10) ______ (ACT) involved for an hour or two.

sections.title: B: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
We didn't have a birthday party for Jake; we took him to the cinema instead.
instead
We took Jake to the cinema for his birthday ______ a party.

It doesn't surprise me that Sandra wants to live in Hollywood.
just
It ______ want to live in Hollywood.

I'm sure the audience will go wild when you come on stage.
bound
The audience ______ wild when you come on stage.

You shouldn't take food into the theatre.
supposed
You ______ food into the theatre.

At least he said he was sorry that he'd ruined my joke!
apologised
At least he ______ my joke!

My parents don't mind you staying with us after the concert.
happy
My parents ______ stay with us after the concert.

Don't tease Libby as she doesn't like it.
fun
Don't ______ Libby as she doesn't like it.

That play really affected me.
impression
That play really ______ me.

sections.title: C: Write a phrasal verb in the correct form to replace the words in bold
sections.instructions: Write a phrasal verb in the correct form to replace the words in bold.

items.sentence: My dad **fell asleep** during the film, as usual!
I felt so **disappointed** when my parents didn't come to watch me in the school play.
Although they're partners in a comedy act, apparently they don't **have a good relationship** with each other off stage.
Let's **perform** a play at the end of term!
Fiona **resembles** her mother; they both love playing board games!
I told Marcie that the word 'stupid' didn't appear in the dictionary and she **believed** it!
Laurel and Hardy will **be remembered** as one of the greatest double acts in comedy history.
You can always **trust** Uncle Colin to cheer you up when you're feeling miserable.
______ an Oscar last year, she's now one of the most powerful movie stars in the industry.
'Which cinema did you go to?' 'The one ______ they don't sell any popcorn!'
That's the actor ______ autograph I got last year!
______ to change his name by his agent, John Smith became Marvo The Magnificent overnight.
The book ______ she's most famous is Dance of the Dinosaurs.
Lyle Lovett, ______ I last saw perform over ten years ago, is on tour again.
And the song ______ by our listeners as their favourite of the week is Goodbye Baby by The Tunesmiths.
'You didn't get into Harvard, did you?' 'No, and that's the reason ______ I ended up going to drama college!'
Could you ______ in the paper and see what's on TV tonight?
I'm reading a ______ set in Greece during the Second World War.
Harry kept us ______ throughout the journey with his non-stop jokes.
I never read ______ of films as I don't trust the critics' opinions.
How did your ______ go? Do you think you'll be offered the main part?
I'd love to work in show ______. It's so glamorous!
Joe told me a joke today but I didn't ______ it at all.
I didn't like this album at first but it's really starting to ______ on me now.
